behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all paths (shared.ts, sqlite.ts, mysql.ts, postgres.ts, test/CLAUDE.md), the exact function signature, required destructured names, exact error message, and the import pattern.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the bug and required API surface but not the internal implementation of the filter function.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert specific literal import pattern `require("internal/sql/shared")`, destructured names `definedColumns`/`columnsSql`, and a function with generic `<T>` signature and specific return shape — though the instruction mandates these, so alternative valid fixes are constrained; additionally the behavioral extraction uses regex that requires specific TypeScript structure (`function <T>`) which a correct arrow-function implementation would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests extract the function source and execute it via node subprocess with multiple behavioral cases.

test_not_tautological.reason: Behavioral tests check 6 distinct input/output pairs including the data-loss fix; a stub returning empty/default would fail T1, T3, T5, T6.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover single/bulk inserts, undefined filtering, SQL format, all three adapters, and CLAUDE.md docs requirement.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass) and descriptive function names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Return object schema `{definedColumns, columnsSql}` is explicitly documented in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names in instruction match those used in tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the files to modify which is appropriate for this API-refactor task; no line numbers given.
